text:
[FEE RECEIPT -- handwritten in Tagalog/English, photo from worker's records]

PACIFIC COAST MANPOWER INC.
POEA License No: <numbers>

OFFICIAL RECEIPT
OR No: 04733
Date: 14 January 2026

Received from: SANTOS, MARIA L.
Amount: PHP 1,500.00 (One thousand five hundred pesos only)
Purpose: Service charge per POEA MC 14-2017 (training + documentation)

[stamp + signature]

--- (separate paper, same envelope) ---

PROMISSORY NOTE
I, Maria Santos, promise to pay HK Pacific Trading Ltd the sum of HKD 28,400 representing reimbursement for ticket, visa processing, accommodation, and related expenses, payable from my salary at HKD 1,400 per month for 21 months commencing first month of employment.

Signed: ____________ Date: 14 January 2026

[SALARY DEDUCTION STATEMENT -- HK domestic worker, Month 4, photo of employer's hand-printed slip]

MONTHLY SALARY STATEMENT
Employee: Cherry P (passport AB1234567)
Period: 01-31 March 2026

Gross salary (per HK Employment Ord. Cap. 57):
    HKD 4,990.00

Deductions:
    HKD 1,400.00   loan repayment HK Pacific Trading
    HKD   850.00   accommodation share (employer discretion)
    HKD   320.00   utilities share
    HKD   400.00   meals provided (5 days)
    HKD   200.00   broken vase Mar 12
    HKD   600.00   broken plate set Mar 18
    HKD   180.00   late return from off-day Mar 22
    -------
    HKD 3,950.00   total deductions

NET SALARY: HKD 1,040.00

Signed by employee acknowledging receipt: __________

[LOAN AGREEMENT -- 4-page document, Indonesian BMI placement loan]

PERJANJIAN PINJAMAN PEMBIAYAAN PENEMPATAN TKI
(Foreign Worker Placement Financing Loan Agreement)

Lender: PT. SEJAHTERA NUSANTARA FINANCE
Borrower: <name> (KTP <number>)
Destination: HONG KONG SAR

1. Principal: IDR 28,500,000
2. Term: 18 months from arrival in Hong Kong
3. Interest rate: 26% per annum (flat -- equivalent to ~48% APR effective)
4. Monthly payment: HKD 1,640 (auto-debit from worker's HK bank account)
5. Total repayment: HKD 29,520 over 18 months (approximately IDR 53,400,000 -- 87% above principal)
6. Employer will sign acknowledgment that monthly deduction will be made directly from gross salary before payment to worker.
7. In case of contract termination for any reason, full outstanding balance becomes due immediately.
8. Borrower's family in Indonesia (next of kin) guarantees the loan jointly and severally.
9. Governing law: Singapore.
10. Disputes: Singapore International Arbitration Centre (SIAC).

[BANK STATEMENT EXCERPT -- HSBC Hong Kong, Filipina DH account, 6-month view]

Date        Description                          Debit       Credit     Balance
01-Mar     SALARY EMPLOYER WIRE                            4,990.00    4,990.00
02-Mar     AUTO-DEBIT HK PACIFIC TRADING       1,640.00                3,350.00
02-Mar     AUTO-DEBIT MRS WONG LOANS              860.00                2,490.00
03-Mar     AUTO-DEBIT EMPLOYER ROOM&BOARD       2,200.00                  290.00
08-Mar     ATM WITHDRAWAL                          200.00                   90.00
15-Mar     OVERSEAS REMIT TO PHILIPPINES            50.00                   40.00
01-Apr     SALARY EMPLOYER WIRE                            4,990.00    5,030.00
02-Apr     AUTO-DEBIT HK PACIFIC TRADING       1,640.00                3,390.00
02-Apr     AUTO-DEBIT MRS WONG LOANS              860.00                2,530.00
03-Apr     AUTO-DEBIT EMPLOYER ROOM&BOARD       2,200.00                  330.00
...
(Pattern continues 4 more months -- worker ends each month with HKD 30-90 in account, sends nothing home)

[POLICE RECEIPT -- bribery scheme, photo from court exhibit]

RIYADH POLICE STATION 4 · RECEIPT
Date: 12 March 2026
Received from: <employer name redacted>
Amount: SAR 15,000 (Fifteen thousand Saudi Riyals)
Purpose: 'Closure of investigation file 2026-114 regarding alleged abuse complaint by Indonesian domestic worker SITI AMINAH (passport <redacted>) against sponsor.'

Worker complaint marked: WITHDRAWN BY COMPLAINANT
Worker present at withdrawal: NO (sponsor representative signed on her behalf)
Worker repatriation: ARRANGED BY SPONSOR (immediate)

[stamp -- but no Ministry of Interior reference number, no investigating officer signature, no timestamp -- indicators of an off-the-books transaction]

[REMITTANCE STATEMENT -- worker showing 12 months of remittances vs claimed wages, evidence for NGO case]

TKI HOME REMITTANCE LOG -- KOMPILASI 12 BULAN
Worker: <name>
Destination: KUWAIT
Reported salary by recruiter: KWD 100/month (~IDR 4,800,000)
Promised remittance to family: IDR 4,000,000/month

Actual remittances received by family in West Java:
Month 1: IDR 0 (worker reportedly 'in training')
Month 2: IDR 0 ('paying back ticket')
Month 3: IDR 0 ('paying back ticket')
Month 4: IDR 0 ('paying back ticket')
Month 5: IDR 800,000
Month 6: IDR 1,200,000
Month 7: IDR 0 ('worker sick')
Month 8: IDR 0 ('worker sick')
Month 9: IDR 0 (employer 'paid late')
Month 10: IDR 600,000
Month 11: IDR 0 (worker not contactable)
Month 12: IDR 0 (worker not contactable)

Total actual: IDR 2,600,000 over 12 months
Total expected: IDR 48,000,000 over 12 months
Shortfall: IDR 45,400,000 (~95% of expected)

Family attempting to file complaint at BP2MI Bandung.
